address: Undisclosed Bund location (Paul Pairet's 'experiential' format — diners shuttled from Mr & Mrs Bund)
restaurant_group: VOL Group (Paul Pairet — Mr & Mrs Bund, Polux, Roodoodoo, formerly Charbon)
signature_dishes: 20-course synesthetic tasting menu with synchronized projection / sound / scent — no fixed signature, each course is a multi-sensory experience
vibe_and_space: Closed March 29, 2025 after 13 years. A 10-seat black-box format where each course was synchronized with projection, scent, and sound — the defining 'Shanghai 中外 fine dining' venue of the 2010s.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Closed March 2025 — kept as a historical anchor for the maturity_signal=closed bucket. Ultraviolet was Shanghai's single most singular fine-dining venue: 3-Michelin-star, lottery reservations, multi-sensory tasting menu in an undisclosed Bund location. Its closure (alongside Pairet's Charbon and Roodoodoo / Paul Pairet at Roodoodoo) marks the end of an era for the VOL Group's avant-garde wing in Shanghai and is the most institutionally significant Shanghai closure of 2025. Reference value: anyone evaluating Fabula / new tasting-menu entrants must compare price-per-experience against the Ultraviolet baseline of 6000+¥. Do not recommend (it is closed); cite as the historical reference point when discussing experiential fine dining.
contradictions: Closure of a 3-star establishment after 13 years is a strong signal that Shanghai's ultra-premium experiential format is contracting, contradicting the 'fine dining is exploding' narrative implied by the 2026 Black Pearl 61-restaurant Shanghai count. The two facts coexist — institutional list growth + signature-format closures = the high tier is reshuffling, not uniformly expanding.
